title: ranunculus pseudacutiusculus
intro: Ranunculus pseudacutiusculus, also known as the Western Buttercup or Small-flowered Buttercup, is a flowering plant species in the buttercup family, Ranunculaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and skin conditions such as eczema and acne. The medicinal properties of Ranunculus pseudacutiusculus have been attributed to its potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and promoting healing, as well as its antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
